title: Selenipedium longifolium
taxonomy: Selenipedium longifolium is a species of orchid, specifically belonging to the genus Selenipedium within the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the order Asparagales. Selenipedium longifolium is a terrestrial orchid, native to tropical regions. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, it shares characteristics with other orchids, such as a unique reproductive system. Further classification can be broken down into family, genus, and species levels.

intro: Selenipedium longifolium, commonly known as the long-leaved lady's slipper orchid, is a rare and endangered terrestrial orchid native to Central and South America. The health benefits of Selenipedium longifolium are attributed to its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable addition to traditional medicine.
